bids.reasoning: Since this is the opening round of the negotiation, there is no previous offer to influence my bid. Therefore, I will propose the bid that maximizes my utility by selecting the best-valued options for each issue according to my preference context. Time pressure is not yet a factor, so my proposal will aim to give me the highest utility without accounting for any concessions at this point.

At this early stage in the negotiation (t=0.02), there's minimal time pressure to make significant concessions. The opponent's initial offer holds the least utility for me, as it chooses the least favorable options for all issues. My strategy is to maintain a strong position by selecting the highest utility values for all issues according to my preference profile, mirroring my initial offer. This keeps my utility high while clearly signaling the values most important to me.

My initial offers have aimed to maximize my utility by selecting the highest-valued options across all issues. The opponent has consistently countered with values that are the least favorable to me, indicating a preference for those options or possibly testing my willingness to concede. Since the negotiation is still in its early stages (t=0.04), I will maintain a strong position with high-utility values while making slight tradeoffs on the least critical issues to encourage movement from the opponent.

Given the early stage of the negotiation (t=0.06), I aim to maintain a high utility of my offers. The opponent has consistently proposed offers at their highest preference value, which indicates that they are holding firm and expecting concessions from me. My prior offers with 'valueF' across all issues have not been accepted, and there has been no progress or messages to indicate flexibility. To test concession at this point, I will adjust on an issue of lower importance to me, issueB, by selecting 'valueB', which still maintains decent utility for me and might show a willingness to negotiate.

My aim is to continue proposing high-utility offers while observing the opponent's pattern of firm stances on offers with low utility to me. Given that it is still early in the negotiation (t=0.08), and the opponent has not yet deviated from their initial low-utility offer, I will maintain a high-utility stance but experiment with further small concessions on lower-weight issues. I have shifted issueB to a value that is less optimal but still reasonably high for me, hoping to see any change in the opponent's response.

At this stage, I aim to offer a bid close to my highest utility while considering the need for some concession to encourage negotiation progress. I will continue to incrementally adjust my offer to maintain strong utility for myself while hinting at a possible middle ground. It's early in the negotiation, so I will build on my previous offer, slightly improving concessions particularly where my utility is least impacted.
